title: NOC 65220 – Pet groomers and animal care workers | GTR Immigration
description: Pet groomers and animal care workers under NOC 65220 are the dedicated professionals ensuring the health, hygiene, and well-being of Canada’s diverse animal population. As a TEER 5 occupation, this role is a highly accessible entry point for international workers who possess a passion for animals and strong manual dexterity. Whether working in high-end grooming salons, veterinary clinics, or wildlife centers, these workers are essential to Canada’s multi-billion dollar pet industry. In 2026, this occupation is a key target for Regional Immigration Pilots and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) that prioritize personal service and animal welfare.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group652 – Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6522 – Support occupations in personal servicesNOC Code65220

duties: Prepare food and feed animals, fish, or birds at scheduled intervals.
Clean and disinfect cages, pens, aquariums, and surrounding areas to ensure animal health.
Shampoo, clip, and groom animals according to breed standards or owner preferences.
Assist Veterinarians and Animal Health Technologists to inoculate and treat animals.
Monitor and document animal behavior and health status for medical or research records.
Train dogs to obey commands and perform specific duties in response to signals.
Assist scientists and researchers in conducting laboratory tests with animals.
Assist animal breeders to handle and nurture animals.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided for most animal care roles.
Training courses in animal handling, grooming, or dog training are usually required for specialized positions.
Animal health or veterinary assistant college courses may be required for specific animal care roles.
Certification from a reputable training school (e.g., for pet trainers) is often an asset.
Physical stamina and the ability to lift up to 20kg are mandatory for many kennel and zoo roles.
salary_prospects: Note: Self-employed pet groomers often have significantly higher earning potential based on their client volume.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in job openings due to the expansion of the "Pet Parents" demographic and the retirement of the existing workforce. As animal welfare standards continue to rise in Canada, the need for professional attendants in shelters, zoos, and clinics will remain a high priority for local labor markets.
labour_market_demand: The demand for animal care workers is Good to Very Good across Canada. In 2026, the industry is seeing a significant surge in demand in British Columbia (Lower Mainland), Ontario (Greater Toronto Area), and Alberta. As pet ownership rates reach record highs, grooming establishments and animal hospitals are facing a shortage of skilled attendants. Employers are actively seeking workers to manage the daily care and aesthetic maintenance of domestic pets and laboratory animals.
High Accessibility: Minimal formal education requirements allow you to secure a job offer and begin your Canadian career quickly.
On-the-Job Training: Many employers provide specialized training in animal behavior, restraint techniques, and grooming standards.
Diverse Work Environments: Choose from roles in pet shops, zoos, animal shelters, or even become self-employed as a mobile groomer.
Path to Specialization: Use this experience to transition into higher-TEER roles such as Animal Health Technologist (NOC 32104) with further education.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A popular route for those working in personal services for at least 9 months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta in eligible service sectors.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 5 workers with job offers from designated employers in the Maritimes.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RCIP): Community-led pathways targeting essential service workers in smaller regions.
